title: Podman machine is running but error while pulling the images
body:
### Issue Description

Describe your issue

my podman machine is active and running. But when I'm doing pull the images using podman command i'm getting like

```
$ podman pull alpine
Error: failed to connect: dial tcp 127.0.0.1:60219: connectex: No connection could be made because the target machine actively refused it.
```

wsl -l -v

```
$ wsl -l -v
  NAME                      STATE           VERSION
* wsl-vpnkit                Running         2
 podman-machine-default    Running         2

```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue

using command prompt and podman desktop, i have tried to pull image but its not working.

### Describe the results you received

$ podman pull alpine
Error: failed to connect: dial tcp 127.0.0.1:60219: connectex: No connection could be made because the target machine actively refused it.

### Describe the results you expected

Describe the results you expected

### podman info output

```yaml
If you are unable to run podman info for any reason, please provide the podman version, operating system and its version and the architecture you are running.
```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: Did you try to stop the machine then start it again?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

~~im having a similar issue but just trying to list containers?~~
```
PS C:\Users\sas_2> podman ps
Error: failed to connect: dial tcp 127.0.0.1:61902: connectex: No connection could be made because the target machine actively refused it.
PS C:\Users\sas_2>
```

EDIT: found my issue, i had previously been playing with rootless mode
destroyed the machine then recreated it and worked again
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Closing as I never heard back.
